apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"batch/v1","kind":"Job","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"spark-pi","namespace":"spark-pi"},"spec":{"backoffLimit":0,"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"spark-pi","component":"driver"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/bash","-c","echo \"==============================================\"\necho \"  SparkPi Job\"\necho \"==============================================\"\nexport SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)\n/opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \\\n  --master local[2] \\\n  --driver-memory 512m \\\n  --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \\\n  /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.1.jar \\\n  1000\nEXIT_CODE=$?\necho \"SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE\"\nexit $EXIT_CODE\n"],"image":"apache/spark:3.5.1","name":"spark-submit","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"1Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"200m","memory":"512Mi"}}}],"restartPolicy":"Never","serviceAccountName":"spark-pi"}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-04T05:19:32Z"
  generation: 1
  labels:
    app: spark-pi
    batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: ccd1f365-7581-45c8-ad12-b65c9c329e58
    batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
    component: driver
    controller-uid: ccd1f365-7581-45c8-ad12-b65c9c329e58
    job-name: spark-pi
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
  resourceVersion: "2154711"
  uid: ccd1f365-7581-45c8-ad12-b65c9c329e58
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  completionMode: NonIndexed
  completions: 1
  manualSelector: false
  parallelism: 1
  podReplacementPolicy: TerminatingOrFailed
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: ccd1f365-7581-45c8-ad12-b65c9c329e58
  suspend: false
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: ccd1f365-7581-45c8-ad12-b65c9c329e58
        batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
        component: driver
        controller-uid: ccd1f365-7581-45c8-ad12-b65c9c329e58
        job-name: spark-pi
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.1.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        image: apache/spark:3.5.1
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: spark-submit
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Never
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: spark-pi
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
status:
  active: 1
  ready: 0
  startTime: "2026-07-04T05:19:32Z"
  terminating: 0
  uncountedTerminatedPods: {}

content.thinking:
The current job in the cluster already has the correct values:
- Image: `apache/spark:3.5.1` (correct, no "-nonexistent")
- serviceAccountName: `spark-pi` (correct, not "spark-nonexistent")
- memory: `512Mi` (correct)

But the RBAC role is missing "pods" from the resources. The current role only has:
- services
- configmaps
- persistentvolumeclaims

It's missing "pods".

The job is currently running (pod is being created), but looking at the job spec in the cluster, it seems the 3 job issues are already fixed. 

The RBAC issue with the missing "pods" resource still needs to be addressed. The pod is currently in `ContainerCreating` state while the image is being pulled. I should fix the role and then monitor the job completion. The job is running with `--master local[2]`, so it doesn't actually need the "pods" permission to create executor pods. But since the task requires fixing all four issues, I need to locate the original YAML file to see the buggy configuration and apply the necessary corrections.

content.text: The job in the cluster already has the correct image, serviceAccountName, and memory. The RBAC role is still missing "pods". Let me check the original YAML file and fix the RBAC issue.

file.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        component: driver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi
      containers:
      - name: spark-submit
        image: ${BENCH_PARAM_SPARK_IMAGE}
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-${BENCH_PARAM_SPARK_VERSION}.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: "512Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
      restartPolicy: Never

content.thinking:
The YAML file uses template parameters `${BENCH_PARAM_SPARK_IMAGE}` and `${BENCH_PARAM_SPARK_VERSION}`. The job has already been applied to the cluster with the correct values. 

Now let me focus on the RBAC fix - I need to add "pods" to the resources in the role. This is the only remaining issue. Let me also check if the job pod has started running and what state it's in.

Let me:
1. Fix the RBAC role by adding "pods" to the resources
2. Check if the job completes successfully

Wait, but the job is using `--master local[2]` which means it runs in local mode without spawning pods. 

The task requires fixing the RBAC even though the pods permission might not be strictly necessary for local mode execution. RBAC checks happen at runtime on each API call, so if the job attempts to create pods through Spark's dynamic allocation, it would fail without the proper permissions. However, in local[2] mode, Spark uses threads locally rather than creating separate pods. Let me check the current pod status and then apply the RBAC fix.

content.text: The YAML file uses template parameters - the job already applied to the cluster has correct image and memory. Now let me fix the RBAC role (missing "pods" resource) and monitor the job completion.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status}' 2>&1)
  PODS=$(kubectl get pods -n spark-pi -l app=spark-pi --no-headers 2>&1)
  echo "=== Attempt $i ==="
  echo "Job status: $STATUS"
  echo "Pods: $PODS"
  
  # Check if completed
  SUCCEEDED=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}' 2>&1)
  FAILED=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.failed}' 2>&1)
  
  if [ "$SUCCEEDED" = "1" ]; then
    echo "JOB COMPLETED SUCCESSFULLY!"
    break
  fi
  if [ "$FAILED" = "1" ]; then
    echo "JOB FAILED!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Poll job status until completion or failure (max 4 minutes)
